semantic.role: Architectural governance document defining layering constraints for the ArchSpine project
responsibilities: Defining and enforcing a strict layered architecture (CLI → services → core/tasks/engines → infra)
Specifying six rules with scopes, constraints, severity levels, and rationale
Maintaining clear separation of concerns across all layers to ensure reusability and testability
Guiding contributors on where to place new code and what dependencies are allowed
outOfScope: General project onboarding or workflow descriptions

English.purpose: To define and enforce a clean layered architecture (CLI → services → core/tasks/engines → infra) for the ArchSpine project, ensuring separation of concerns, reusability, and maintainability.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for all developers contributing to the ArchSpine repository. It describes the architectural constraints that must be followed when adding or modifying code in each layer. The rules are defined with severity (Error or Warning) to indicate strictness and provide clear guidance for code reviews and refactoring.
key_takeaways: The CLI layer must remain thin and only handle entrypoints and command adapters, without absorbing pipeline or persistence logic.
Services orchestrate runtime but must not place view-specific logic in infra; service subsystems should be organized under src/services/view/.
Core modules define pipeline contracts and must not depend on CLI entry code.
Task modules implement stage-local work on top of core and engines, avoiding CLI parsing or unrelated orchestration.
Engines provide reusable analysis and transformation logic and must not depend on CLI or service-level orchestration.
Infra modules expose stable low-level facades; callers should prefer public facades over private implementation paths.
